fields.helpText: The value of this field is the name of the UID. (A UID is an Unique IDentifier.) Names of UIDs are defined in the DICOM standard.

The value of this field is a numeric code that uniquely identifies an object. The code consists of an indeterminate number of integer numbers, separated by dots. The DICOM standard defines a number of such unique identifiers; vendors and other organizations may create their own unique identifiers by requesting an 'UID-root' from their national standards organization.
The value of this field is a code that specifies whether this UID is a 'normal' UID, or a Meta UID. A Meta-UID has Sub-UIDs.
